User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the iGen application, including their profile details, role, and application-specific settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for login and communication.

role.description: The role of the user within the application, determining access privileges.

hasClaimedCredits.description: Indicates whether the user has claimed their initial $300 iGen Cloud Credits.

apiKey.description: The Google Cloud API Key provided by the user to power AI services.

preferredLanguage.description: The user's preferred language for the application UI (e.g., 'VI' for Vietnamese, 'EN' for English).

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was last updated.

Feature.title: Feature
Feature.description: Defines an AI feature available in the iGen ecosystem dashboard.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Feature entity.

name.description: The name of the feature (e.g., 'Rendering', 'Video Creator').

description.description: A brief description of what the feature offers.

thumbnailUrl.description: URL to a thumbnail image or icon representing the feature in the dashboard.

iconKey.description: A key or name corresponding to an icon from the Lucide React library, used for UI representation.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a user-created project leveraging a specific iGen AI feature, storing its settings and outputs.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Project entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this project. (Relationship: User 1:N Project)

featureId.description: Reference to the Feature used for this project. (Relationship: Feature 1:N Project)

name.description: The name given to the project by the user.

description.description: An optional description provided by the user for the project.

mode.description: The operational mode chosen for the project (e.g., 'SPEED_MODE' or 'PRO_MODE').

settings.description: A JSON string containing feature-specific settings and parameters for this project (e.g., 'Consistent Character Sync', 'Cinematic Pan').

status.description: The current processing status of the project (e.g., 'draft', 'processing', 'completed', 'failed').

outputUrl.description: URL to the generated output file or result of the project, if applicable.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the project was created. Used for a 30-day TTL (Time-To-Live) automatic deletion policy.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the project was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is restricted to the authenticated user whose UID matches 'userId' in the path. Includes a 'role' field for descriptive purposes, but admin authorization is managed via the separate '/admins' collection.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier (UID) of the user, matching 'request.auth.uid'.

definition.description: Stores definitions of available AI features. This collection is publicly readable by all authenticated users to populate the ecosystem dashboard. No specific authorization context is denormalized.

params.name: featureId
params.description: The unique identifier of the AI feature.

definition.description: Stores individual projects created by a specific user. Access is restricted to the owner (`userId`). These documents are automatically deleted by Firestore after 30 days based on a TTL policy on the 'createdAt' field.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier (UID) of the user who owns this project.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the project.

definition.description: A collection storing the UIDs of users who have 'Admin' privileges. The *existence* of a document at `/admins/$(request.auth.uid)` grants admin status, following the DBAC 'Existence over Content' principle. Documents can be empty or contain minimal user info.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier (UID) of a user with administrator privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and highly debuggable, strictly adhering to the core design principles and mandates. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL)** is achieved through several strategies:
1.  **Path-Based Ownership:** For user-specific data like profiles (`/users/{userId}`) and projects (`/users/{userId}/projects/{projectId}`), the `userId` is embedded directly into the document path. This allows security rules to check `request.auth.uid == userId` without needing any `get()` operations on parent documents. The `Project` documents also explicitly include a `userId` field, serving as a denormalized authorization context for invariant checks (e.g., `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId`).
2.  **Global Roles:** For global roles like 'admin', a dedicated collection (`/admins/{adminId}`) is used. Authorization is determined by the *existence* of a document at `/admins/$(request.auth.uid)`, eliminating the need to read and evaluate fields from a user's profile document or perform `get()` calls to other parts of the database. This pattern ensures atomicity and simplifies rule logic.

**Clarity of Intent (Debuggability)** is paramount. Collection names (`users`, `features`, `projects`, `admins`) and wildcards (`{userId}`, `{featureId}`, `{projectId}`, `{adminId}`) are semantically clear and descriptive. This makes authorization rules explicit; for example, rules on `/users/{userId}/projects/{projectId}` clearly state that the authenticated user must match the `userId` in the path, reflecting direct ownership.

**DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control)** is implemented by storing role information directly in Firestore. The `/admins/{adminId}` collection serves as the authoritative source for determining administrative privileges, without relying on custom claims or client-side logic for access decisions. The `role` field on the `User` document is considered descriptive and not used for direct authorization of other documents.

**QAPs (Queries Are Not Filters)** are fully supported by this structure:
*   **User Profiles (`/users/{userId}`):** Users can only query their own profile (`allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId;`). Admins could query all, but typical user interaction is single-document `get` or `list` for self. 
*   **Features (`/features/{featureId}`):** This is a public read-only collection. Any authenticated user can list all features, as all documents within this collection share the same security posture (`allow list: if request.auth.uid != null;`).
*   **Projects (`/users/{userId}/projects/{projectId}`):** A user can only list projects belonging to themselves (`allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId;`). The path inherently scopes the query, making `list` operations secure and efficient without filtering sensitive data.
*   **Admins (`/admins/{adminId}`):** Access to this collection itself is tightly controlled, but the *existence* check for admin status (`exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/admins/$(request.auth.uid))`) does not perform a list operation to filter data, thus adhering to QAPs for authorization checks.

**Structural Segregation** is maintained by ensuring each collection (or subcollection) holds documents with homogeneous security requirements. For instance, all documents in `/features` are publicly readable by authenticated users, and all documents in `/users/{userId}/projects` are owned by `userId`.

**Access Modeling** standardizes on path-based ownership for private and hierarchical user data, and the 'existence' pattern for global roles. There are no collaborative data entities in the current scope, so membership maps are not utilized here.

**Data Clarity and Predictability** is ensured through consistent naming conventions (e.g., `{userId}`, `{projectId}`), the use of a single `status` field for finite state modeling in `Project` documents, and referencing explicit schema definitions.
